mnemonics: Fork It Right (OpenMP) - Think of a fork in the road where multiple paths (threads) are created from a single main path, all sharing the same scenery (memory).
keyPoints: OpenMP uses pragmas (compiler directives like #pragma omp parallel) to easily parallelize loops and code sections in C/C++/Fortran.
It follows a fork-join model: the master thread forks into multiple worker threads to execute code in parallel, then joins back to a single thread.
The 'shared' and 'private' clauses are fundamental for controlling variable scope, preventing race conditions where threads incorrectly access shared data.
commonMistakes: Race Conditions: Forgetting to protect shared variables with critical sections (#pragma omp critical) or atomic operations, leading to unpredictable results.
False Sharing: Performance degradation caused by threads on different cores invalidating each other's cache lines unnecessarily, often due to poorly aligned private data.
